title: Enable waveform dumping via command line
body:
As a recent user of cocotb, the lack of ability to command iverilog to create a dump file with command line arguments has become a rather serious problem.  When simulating, cocotb does not create a top-level verilog file, it simulates the top-level module directly.  The problem is that in this case, enabling waveform dump requires adding code to the actual module, not to the module's testbench.  The cocotb documentation recommends making this selectable based on a define.  The problem is that this does not work if the module contains other modules which have the same dump code added based on the same define.  

The most reasonable fix is to add a --dumpfile parameter or similar that can not only specify the dump file, but also enable dumping for the top-level module.
